hypothesis: If equivariance-inducing training regularization (such as equigrad) causes non-equivariant architectures to converge toward the same representational manifold as architecturally equivariant models, then systematically interpolating the strength of this regularization during training will reveal a quantitative threshold—measurable via CKNNA alignment with equivariant reference models—above which downstream task performance plateaus, thereby establishing representation alignment as a sufficient proxy for architectural symmetry constraints and enabling the design of cheaper models that achieve equivariant-level generalization without equivariant architectures.
